description: Iraq invades Kuwait, prompting fears of major supply disruption from two large OPEC producers and a sharp spike in Brent prices.
Currency and banking crises across East Asia sharply cut regional oil demand, contributing to a prolonged price collapse into 1998.
Terrorist attacks on the US caused a demand-side shock and short-term market panic amid broader economic uncertainty.
US-led invasion of Iraq disrupted output from a major OPEC producer and raised a sustained geopolitical risk premium.
Brent hit an all-time high (about $147/barrel) driven by strong emerging-market demand, a weak US dollar, and speculative activity, just before the global financial crisis.
Collapse of Lehman Brothers triggered a global recession and a historic demand collapse, driving Brent down more than 70 percent within months.
Widespread unrest across the Middle East and North Africa, including the loss of Libyan output, pushed prices sharply higher.
Surging US shale production combined with OPEC's decision in Nov 2014 not to cut output led to a supply glut and a price crash from over $110 to under $50.
OPEC agreed its first production cut since 2008, with non-OPEC allies forming OPEC+, helping stabilize and lift prices from 2016 lows.

event_name: US Withdrawal from Iran Nuclear Deal
description: US re-imposed sanctions on Iranian oil exports, tightening supply expectations and lifting prices through 2018.
Drone and missile strikes temporarily knocked out roughly half of Saudi Arabia's oil production capacity, causing the largest single-day price spike in decades.
OPEC+ talks collapsed; Saudi Arabia launched a price war and ramped up output just as COVID-19 demand collapsed, crashing prices.
WHO's pandemic declaration and subsequent global lockdowns caused an unprecedented collapse in oil demand.
OPEC+ agreed a record cut of about 9.7 million barrels per day to support prices amid the pandemic demand collapse.
Russia's invasion triggered sanctions on a top-3 global oil exporter and a major supply-risk premium, driving Brent above $120 per barrel.

event_name: US/EU Ban on Russian Oil Imports Proposed
description: Announcements of bans and restrictions on Russian oil imports intensified supply concerns and price volatility.
